Thus, we must: (1) replace 'dim' with 'axis' in MindSpore case; (2) ensure indices are valid for both frameworks — for axis=0 on input shape [10,5], indices along dim 0 must be in [0,9]; current index shape [5,5] implies indexing into dim 0, so each index value must be in [0,9]; to avoid -7 error, we constrain indices to valid range (e.g., use dtype int64 but values in [0,9]); (3) add 'reduction="none"' for MindSpore since it's required and default; (4) MindSpore does not support float64 on Ascend/GPU per doc — only float16/float32; thus downgrade dtype to float32 for compatibility; (5) PyTorch scatter supports float64, but to align with MindSpore’s supported dtypes and ensure cross-framework comparability, use float32 for both.
